_doc: Independently-derived golden expectations for the synthetic fixture (GOLDEN_MASTER.md §3). Values traced by hand from V3_ENGINE_LOGIC.md band TABLES, NOT from our engine and NOT from the quarantined v3 (clean-room, ADR-0001). The test asserts the lifted engine reproduces these. Tolerance: band scores exact; RecScore exact to 2dp. NO real data — placeholders only.

price_band._note: edge bid: cov=100, hist=50 (no baseline), z=100 (2-bid group), cont=0; rec = price*.35 + 45

coverage_band._note: edge bid: price=100, hist=50, z=100, cont=0; rec = 55 + cov*.25

historical_band._note: edge bid: price=100, cov=100, z=100, cont=0; rec = 70 + hist*.20

continuity._note: DC60/LT01: incumbent S70 vs cheaper non-incumbent rival S71. Continuity 100 for the incumbent, 0 for the rival.

scenario_c_incumbent_defense._note: DC60/LT01: A picks the cheapest (rival S71); B picks the highest RecScore (incumbent S70, continuity-boosted); C retains the incumbent S70 (within comparable premium at full coverage). Proves A != B and the incumbent-defense lens.

b_fallback._note: All-In blank -> FOB 90 + Del 5 + Veg 3 - Lot 2 - AllLot 1

b_doublesub._note: All-In 95 present WITH Lot_Discount 2 -> NOT re-subtracted (guard)

scenario_d_split._note: DC20/TF1: SupRankScore -> top-2 = {S31 (68), S33 (63)} over S32 (62.75, dragged by its weak LT01 alt). LT03 is coverable only by S32 (outside top-2) -> fallback-flagged fill.

scenario_a_lowest_cost._note: Scenario A picks the cheapest eligible bid per cell. On DC40/LT01 the cheapest eligible is SX0 @ 100.00.

scenario_b_recommended._note: B is the highest-RecScore pick per cell. On DC40/LT01, SX0 (cheaper, same scores) wins.

scenario_e_exclusion._note: SX0 excluded -> E re-runs B minus SX0 -> SX1 wins DC40/LT01.

scenario_f_custom._note: Custom override forces S32 on DC20/LT01 (B picked S31).

scenario_g_preferred._note: Preferred rule forces S32 on DC20/LT01; a second rule for a no-bid supplier on LT99 hits the exception path and keeps B's pick.

concentration._note: S50's Scenario-B RecSpend (6000 cases * 100) is >= 40% of total category B spend -> flagged. Distinct from the per-DC cap.

cap_breach._note: DC11/TF1 awards span >2 suppliers across its lots in Scenario B -> cap_breach_flag set on those B awards.
